patch_description:
Proposes a more parameter-efficient, symmetric ansatz for the 9-qubit tic-tac-toe QML task. Each ansatz block consists of:
- a three-group RY layer with shared parameters across groups (a, b, c) assigned cyclically to wires [0..8],
- an entangling CZ layer on all allowed grid edges (no trainable params),
- a shared-parameter CRZ entangling layer on the same edges (single crz parameter per block).
This design increases expressivity while dramatically reducing per-block parameter count (4 unique parameters per block: a, b, c, crz), promoting better generalization and faster convergence in Adam optimization. Data re-uploading remains l=3 with p=2 repetitions per upload, and the rest of the training/evaluation pipeline is untouched to ensure fair comparison.
